steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to explain Brook Farm's goal using a quotation from George Ripley. The answer must include Ripley's own words that convey his purpose, with enough context to show what the quotation means.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'In a letter, writer George Ripley explained his goal to "guarantee the highest mental freedom."': includes only the opening fragment of the quotation, omitting the rest of Ripley's goal (labor suited to talents, securing the fruits of industry). The partial quote doesn't fully explain the goal, and Brook Farm is not even mentioned. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Utopias, such as Brook Farm, founded by George Ripley in 1841, were based on a specific set of principles intended to create a perfect society.': contains no quotation from Ripley at all, so the task of using his words is not accomplished. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Founded by George Ripley near Boston, Massachusetts, Brook Farm was part of a trend in the 1800s, when reformers in the United States built utopias.': provides background on Brook Farm but includes no quotation and says nothing about Ripley's stated goals. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Established in 1841, Brook Farm was a utopian community created to "guarantee the highest mental freedom, by providing all with labor... [and] the fruits of their industry," according to founder George Ripley.': includes the full substantive quotation from Ripley's letter, attributes it to him, names Brook Farm, and frames the quote as his stated goal. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
